name: Mekhit
description: Mekhit, also known as Menhit, was an ancient Egyptian war goddess whose name means "the slaughterer." She was primarily associated with warfare and protection, particularly the protection of the pharaoh in battle. Mekhit was often depicted as a lioness, reflecting her fierce and aggressive nature. Her role was to defend the pharaoh and the people of Egypt from their enemies, ensuring victory in conflicts. She was considered the consort of Anhur, the god of war and hunting, and together they formed a powerful divine couple, symbolizing the strength and protection offered to the Egyptian kingdom. Mekhit was also associated with the royal cobra, the uraeus, further emphasizing her protective role and connection to the pharaoh. While not as widely worshipped as some other Egyptian deities, Mekhit played a significant role in the religious beliefs surrounding warfare and royal power. Her temples and shrines were often located near those of Anhur, highlighting their close association. Mekhit embodied the fierce and protective aspects of the divine feminine, ensuring the safety and security of Egypt.
subtitle: Lioness Goddess of War and Protection

keyMyths.title: Consort of Anhur
keyMyths.description: Mekhit is known as the consort of Anhur, forming a divine couple representing strength and protection. Together they were believed to provide the pharaoh with victory in battle.

keyMyths.title: The Slaughterer
keyMyths.description: Mekhit's name means 'the slaughterer,' reflecting her role as a fierce war goddess. She would fiercely defend the pharaoh and the people of Egypt from enemies.

keyMyths.title: Lioness Protector
keyMyths.description: As a lioness, Mekhit embodies strength, courage, and protection. Her lioness form symbolizes her ferocity in defending the pharaoh and the kingdom.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
sources.work: Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt

sources.author: George Hart
sources.work: A Dictionary of Egyptian Gods and Goddesses
sources.description: Detailed information about various Egyptian deities.
sources.text: Detailed information about various Egyptian deities.
